notes: Research source: .plans/ideas/autonomous-harness-map-2026-04-18.md; this hub is the live execution queue.
The five existing plan-hub lanes remain authoritative; D.* and T.* stay loop families, not schema changes.
Full-view cleanup requires both route-unreachability and parity review against the live folded surface.
Use targeted bun run test or bun run test as the fast iterative gate; coverage is scheduled or pre-merge evidence, not the default inner loop.
.plans/ is the durable repo truth; any .claude/agent-memory pilot remains environment-local until freshness, expiry, and ownership rules exist.
Baseline refresh report: reports/2026-04-18-baseline-refresh.md; all three verified silent-failure mutation loops are now closed and a bounded shared cleanup loop landed.

The packages/client test-runner stall is closed: full client `bun run test` now exits cleanly after aliasing the EAS SDK test surface and replacing the mock-heavy Cards omnibus with split behavior tests.

Repo-quick validation now passes end to end with packages/admin `bun run test:hub`, the default admin `bun run test` gate now exits cleanly after being aligned with the package contract, the active env/runtime/script Storacha control surfaces are now Pinata-only, and `bun run ipfs:repin:audit -- --chain 42161 --include input --input /dev/null --out /tmp/ipfs-repin-audit-codex.json` completes cleanly; the remaining validation debt is the separately excluded legacy admin view-heavy surface plus historical changelog/reporting references and the deliberate negative regression string in the shared IPFS test.

ui.handoff_note: Design specialist rollout intentionally deferred until Tier 0 partials are closed and a single pilot surface is chosen.

state_api.handoff_note: Tier 0 control-surface work is complete, all three verified silent-failure mutation loops are closed, bounded cleanup loops landed in shared, in the shared/client/agent Storacha runtime path, in the contracts-side Storacha upload path, and in the root repo-script Storacha path, the packages/client runner now exits cleanly, repo-quick passes via admin `test:hub`, the default admin `bun run test` gate now exits cleanly, the shared Lit dev-mode banner, JobQueueProvider, useDraftAutoSave, useActionOperations, useDepositForm, and useHypercertDraft `act(...)` warnings are closed, the repo-owned env/runtime/script Storacha surfaces are now Pinata-only, and the repin audit wrapper runs cleanly; the next blocker to tighten is a dedicated hardening loop for the excluded legacy admin view-heavy surface or a bounded cleanup pass for historical changelog/reporting references.

contracts.handoff_note: No active Solidity scope in the current phase; reopen only if a later loop touches contract artifacts directly.

qa_pass_1.handoff_note: Blocked until the state lane finishes warning-hygiene cleanup or intentionally scopes a dedicated legacy admin view hardening pass, and there is a concrete UI pilot to review.

qa_pass_2.handoff_note: Blocked on QA pass 1 and on deciding the dedicated legacy admin view-heavy hardening path now that the repin validation edge is closed.

history.note: Scaffolded feature hub in active
Promoted the autonomy research map into a live active hub and captured current Tier 0 / Loop A status.
Tier 0 unblockers are mostly complete; remaining work is cleanup baseline refresh, inner-loop policy codification, and memory policy.
Refreshed the cleanup and mutation-bug baseline; the three silent-failure paths remain open and stale dead-code classifications were recorded in the active hub.
Completed the remaining control-surface work by codifying the inner-loop testing policy and memory policy in the plan hub, template notes, and builder docs.
Closed the CreateListingDialog silent-failure loop with a local actionable error fallback and a focused admin regression test; two provider-side mutation bugs remain.
Closed the Work provider silent-failure loop by rethrowing rejected work submissions after debug logging and backing it with a focused provider test; JobQueue is the remaining verified mutation bug.
Closed the JobQueue auto-flush silent-failure loop with a focused provider regression test, leaving no verified silent-failure mutation bugs open in the active baseline.

Landed one bounded shared cleanup loop by removing the deprecated query invalidation shim, then tightened repo-quick blocker evidence to a packages/client test-runner stall with a live fsevents watcher after suite output.

Closed the packages/client runner stall by aliasing the EAS SDK test surface and replacing the mock-heavy Cards omnibus with split behavior tests; repo-quick now reaches packages/admin, where isolated and repo-quick admin Vitest runs hang with the child node idle in uv__io_poll and holding KQUEUE handles.

Aligned repo-quick with packages/admin `test:hub`, confirmed quick now clears shared, client, admin, and agent tests without stalling in the admin legacy suite, and narrowed the remaining quick failure to unrelated format drift in `.plans/backlog/harness-hardening-wave-1/status.json`; the legacy admin view-heavy surface remained a separate hardening target.

Cleared the unrelated backlog status formatting drift, re-ran `node scripts/ci-local.js --quick` to a full pass, and updated the blocker list so repo-quick is no longer treated as blocked; the remaining validation debt was warning-hygiene noise plus the separate legacy admin view-heavy surface.

Aligned the default packages/admin `bun run test` script with the package contract by excluding legacy `src/__tests__/views/**` and `StrategyKernelStep.test.tsx` from the default inner-loop gate, then verified `bun run test`, `bun run test:hub`, and `node scripts/ci-local.js --quick` all pass; warning-hygiene noise remains the primary validation debt.

Closed the CookieJarManageModal Radix dialog warning by adding a localized modal description and regression assertion, verified the focused admin test now runs cleanly without the warning, and kept the remaining warning-hygiene debt narrowed to the other shared/client noise plus the separate legacy admin view-heavy decision.

Closed the shared JobQueueProvider React `act(...)` warning by suppressing no-op queue stats and offline-banner updates, then verified the focused provider test and shared typecheck pass cleanly; the remaining warning-hygiene debt is now narrowed to the other shared/client warning noise plus the separate legacy admin view-heavy decision.

Verified the current shared/client full-suite runs do not reproduce the earlier react-intl warning claim, closed the useDraftAutoSave async `act(...)` warning with a focused shared test fix, and tightened the live warning-hygiene debt to Lit dev-mode noise, Storacha sourcemap output, and the remaining shared hook `act(...)` warnings.

Closed the shared useActionOperations React `act(...)` warning by executing the async hook calls inside `act(...)` in the focused test, then verified the isolated hook suite passes cleanly without the warning; remaining hygiene work stays narrowed to Lit dev-mode noise, Storacha sourcemap output, and the other shared hook `act(...)` surfaces.

Closed the shared useDepositForm React `act(...)` warning by awaiting the `setValue(..., { shouldValidate: true })` flush in the focused test, then verified the isolated hook suite passes cleanly without the warning; remaining hygiene work stays narrowed to Lit dev-mode noise, Storacha sourcemap output, and the other shared hook `act(...)` surfaces.

Closed the shared useHypercertDraft React `act(...)` warning by disabling the unrelated default autoLoad side effect in the draftKey-only test, then verified the isolated hook suite passes cleanly without the warning; remaining hygiene work stays narrowed to Lit dev-mode noise, Storacha sourcemap output, and the other shared hook `act(...)` surfaces.

Re-baselined the full shared suite, confirmed the earlier shared hook act warnings no longer reproduce there, and closed the repeated Lit dev-mode banner by pre-marking Lit's dev-mode warning code in the shared test setup; the remaining repeated stderr blocker is now primarily the Storacha sourcemap noise.

Removed the Storacha runtime path from shared/client/agent by switching shared IPFS uploads to Pinata-only, deleting the stale agent media service, and dropping the shared/client test-runner Storacha dependency inlining; focused shared validation no longer reproduces the Storacha sourcemap noise, and the remaining Storacha work is now a broader purge across contracts, repo scripts, docs, and compatibility strings.

Removed the contracts-side Storacha upload path by switching the live deploy/repair scripts in packages/contracts to Pinata-only, removing @storacha/client from packages/contracts/package.json, and updating the indexer default IPFS gateway; the remaining Storacha work is now concentrated in the root repo scripts, env/docs truth, and backward-compat strings.

Removed the root repo-script Storacha path by converting the root upload and repin helpers to Pinata-only flows, dropping root @storacha/client, and validating the uploader dry-run plus targeted script-surface typecheck; the remaining Storacha work is now narrowed to autogenerated env truth and the shared legacy gateway alias, while the repo-owned repin wrapper still hangs in varlock bootstrap before the script body runs.

Aligned .env.schema and env.d.ts to Pinata-only repo truth without editing the operator-local root .env, removed the shared legacy gateway alias from the IPFS client, added focused Pinata/retired-alias regression coverage, and fixed the repin wrapper so the direct ipfs:repin:audit smoke now completes.
